changes: Rewrote verbose original SKILL.md into concise, schema-focused format with exact JSON output schemas for both task types
Added explicit classification rules for prioritize-tasks (must_do_today, deferred, should_do, nice_to_have) with concrete criteria
Clarified that deferred classification ignores effort level (high effort + low impact + far deadline = still deferred)
Updated skill description/frontmatter to cover both scheduling AND prioritization use cases, ensuring the bare agent loads the skill for both task types
key_insight: The bare runtime requires models to explicitly load skills via <load-skill>. The original description 'Generate structured daily plans' did not trigger skill loading for the prioritize-tasks task. Broadening the description to include 'categorizing/prioritizing tasks' was the critical fix that raised prioritize-tasks from 95 to 100.
